public_reputation: Manager of reserve goods, granary order, and ration-ready storage in a harvest estate context.
appearance: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr carries the marks of storehouse matron in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a storehouse matron whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Harvest Haven.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of storehouse matron in and around Harvest Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Harvest Haven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Harvest Haven.
Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a storehouse matron.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr is readable as a storehouse matron tied to Harvest Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Haven: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr presents as someone shaped by the duties of a storehouse matron and the daily pressures of Harvest Haven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a storehouse matron, and proof that strangers will not make Harvest Haven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a storehouse matron too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr always knows more about Harvest Haven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to storehouse matron duties in Harvest Haven.
social_texture: Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Harvest Haven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Storehouse touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Storehouse Matron Ilyse Marr when the party needs a socially grounded face for Harvest Haven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
